info.title: Platform - Bulk Export
info.description: The Bulk Export APIs are used to integrate external systems and extract specific data from Cookie Consent and Universal Consent & Preference Management in the OneTrust platform.

hostname.description: The OneTrust hostname such as app.onetrust.com, app-eu.onetrust.com, app-de.onetrust.com, app-uk.onetrust.com, app-apac.onetrust.com, trial.onetrust.com, or uat.onetrust.com.

tags.name: Bulk Export
tags.description: The Bulk Export APIs are used to extract large volumes of cookie receipts, consent receipts, and data subjects.

get.description:
Use this API to retrieve a list of bulk exports. The response will include the created date, ID, and status of each export.

> 🗒 Things to Know
> 
> - The `status` of a bulk export will automatically change to `Expired` 10 days after its creation.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: The page number of the results.

schema.description: Results page to be retrieved (0..N). Example- '?page=1'

parameters.name: size
parameters.description: The number of results per page.

schema.description: Number of records per page (1..2000). Example- '?size=30'

parameters.name: sort
parameters.description: The sort criteria that dictates the order of the results.

schema.description: The sort criteria that dictates the order of the results.

get.description:
Use this API to retrieve bulk export credit details for the account. The response will include the total number of credits available for the account along with the number of credits remaining and the number of credits used.

> 🗒 Things to Know
> 
> - Each account is limited to 20 bulk exports per month. If you cancel a bulk export before it completes, it will not be counted towards this limit. Please contact OneTrust Support or your Customer Success Manager (CSM) to increase your export limit.

Use this API to retrieve the current status and progress of a bulk export.

> 🗒 Things to Know

get.description:
Use this API to retrieve the download details for a bulk export. The response will include the authorization token (expires after 1 hour), file name, and URL that you can include within the following cURL command to download the export files:

```curl
curl --header "Authorization: Bearer <TOKEN_FROM_RESPONSE>" --output "<FILENAME_FROM_RESPONSE>" -fL "URL_FROM_RESPONSE"
```

> 🗒 Things to Know
> 
> - Your export may generate multiple files to preserve download speeds. Each file name and URL will contain a unique authorization token.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The unique identifier for the bulk export.

schema.description: The unique identifier for the bulk export.

get.summary: Get List of Bulk Export Download Details
get.description:
Use this paginated API to retrieve the download details for a bulk export. The response will include the authorization token (expires after 1 hour), file name, and URL that you can include within the following cURL command to download the export files:

```curl
curl --header "Authorization: Bearer <TOKEN_FROM_RESPONSE>" --output "<FILENAME_FROM_RESPONSE>" -fL "URL_FROM_RESPONSE"
```

> 🗒 Things to Know
> 
> - Your export may generate multiple files to preserve download speeds. Each file name and URL will contain a unique authorization token.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The unique identifier for the bulk export.

schema.description: The unique identifier for the bulk export.

parameters.name: continuationToken
parameters.description: The continuation token for retrieving the next set of records.

schema.description: Continuation Token for fetching the next set of records.

parameters.name: size
parameters.description: The maximum number of records to be retrieved.

schema.description: Maximum number of records to be fetched.

429.description:
Too Many Requests. 
For more information, see [API Rate Limits](https://developer.onetrust.com/onetrust/reference/rate-limits-overview).

schema.description: The number of seconds after which requests will be allowed again.
The unit of time for which the rate limit applies
The unique identifier for the rate-limiting event.
The number of requests made within the specified period.
The number of requests allowed within the specified period.

name.description: The name of the bulk export.

exportType.description: The type of data to export. Different export types support different filtering parameters.

startDate.description: The start of a date range used to filter results. This parameter is required for the `CONSENT_RECEIPTS`, `COOKIE_RECEIPTS`, `DATA_SUBJECTS` and `DATA_SUBJECTS_BULK_ADD_PURPOSE` export types, and the timespan is limited to 180 days. This parameter is optional for the `DATA_SUBJECTS_BULK_ACTIONS` export type.

endDate.description: The end of a date range used to filter results. This parameter is required for the `CONSENT_RECEIPTS`, `COOKIE_RECEIPTS`, `DATA_SUBJECTS` and `DATA_SUBJECTS_BULK_ADD_PURPOSE` export types, and must be within 180 days from the `startDate`. This parameter is optional for the `DATA_SUBJECTS_BULK_ACTIONS` export type. The `endDate` can be as recent as yesterday, but cannot be the current day.

exportFormat.description: The file format of the bulk export.

exportType.description: The type of data to export.

startDate.description: The start of a date range used to filter results. This parameter is required for the `CONSENT_RECEIPTS` export type, and the timespan is limited to 180 days.

endDate.description: The end of a date range used to filter results. This parameter is required for the `CONSENT_RECEIPTS` export type and must be within 180 days from the `startDate`. The `endDate` can be as recent as yesterday, but cannot be the current day.

params.description: The parameters used to filter results. To define multiple parameters, click the Add Object option that appears below the first object.

exportType.description: The type of data to export.

startDate.description: The start of a date range used to filter results. This parameter is required for the `COOKIE_RECEIPTS` export type, and the timespan is limited to 180 days.

endDate.description: The end of a date range used to filter results. This parameter is required for the `COOKIE_RECEIPTS` export type and must be within 180 days from the `startDate`. The `endDate` can be as recent as yesterday, but cannot be the current day.

params.description: The parameters used to filter results. To define multiple parameters, click the Add Object option that appears below the first object.

exportType.description: The type of data to export.

startDate.description: The start of a date range used to filter results. This parameter is optional for the `DATA_SUBJECTS_BULK_ACTIONS` export type.

endDate.description: The end of a date range used to filter results. This parameter is optional for the `DATA_SUBJECTS_BULK_ACTIONS` export type.

params.description: The parameters used to filter results. To define multiple parameters, click the Add Object option that appears below the first object.

exportType.description: The type of data to export.

startDate.description: The start of a date range used to filter results. This parameter is required for the `DATA_SUBJECTS_BULK_ADD_PURPOSE` export type, and the timespan is limited to 180 days.

endDate.description: The end of a date range used to filter results. This parameter is required for the `DATA_SUBJECTS_BULK_ADD_PURPOSE` export type and must be within 180 days from the `startDate`. The `endDate` can be as recent as yesterday, but cannot be the current day.

params.description: The parameters used to filter results. To define multiple parameters, click the Add Object option that appears below the first object.

exportType.description: The type of data to export.

startDate.description: The start of a date range used to filter results. This parameter is required for the `DATA_SUBJECTS` export type, and the timespan is limited to 180 days.

endDate.description: The end of a date range used to filter results. This parameter is required for the `DATA_SUBJECTS` export type and must be within 180 days from the `startDate`. The `endDate` can be as recent as yesterday, but cannot be the current day.

params.description: The parameters used to filter results. To define multiple parameters, click the Add Object option that appears below the first object.

name.description: This parameter filters results by the bulk action type.

values.description: The bulk action type. Exactly 1 item is required. This is a mandatory filter.

name.description: This parameter filters results by a specific identifier type, such as email.

values.description: The type of data subject identifier used as an additional identifier.

name.description: This parameter filters results by a specific collection point ID.

values.description: The unique identifier of the collection point.

name.description: This parameter filters results by the collection point JWT.

values.description: The collection point JWT to use for bulk import. Exactly 1 item is required.

name.description: This parameter filters results by collection point versions.

values.description: The version of the collection point.

name.description: This parameter filters results by custom preference names.

values.description: The name of the custom preference. If the custom preference name is included without the custom preference value, the export will return all records with the custom preference.

name.description: This parameter filters results by the custom preference value.

values.description: The value of the custom preference. This parameter must be used in conjunction with the custom preference name. The export will return all records with the custom preference and the specific value.

name.description: This parameter filters results by data element names.

values.description: The name of the data element. If the data element name is included without the data element value, the export will return all records with the data element.

name.description: This parameter filters results by data element values.

values.description: The value of the data element. This parameter must be used in conjunction with the data element name. The export will return all records with the data element and the specific value.

name.description: This parameter filters results by various dates recorded for a data subject.

values.description: The type of date that the `startDate` and `endDate` reference to filter results. The `CREATED_DATE` value filters results by data subjects' `FirstTransactionDate`. The `LAST_MODIFIED_DATE` value filters results by data subjects' `LastTransactionDate`. The `LAST_INTERACTION_DATE` value filters results by data subjects' `LastInteractionDate`. This parameter defaults to `CREATED_DATE`. Exactly 1 selection is required.

name.description: This parameter filters results by the target purpose status when the source purpose status is not found.

values.description: The target purpose status when the source purpose is not found. Exactly 1 item is required.

name.description: This parameter filters results by test data subjects.

values.description: This flag indicates whether to include or exclude test data subjects. This parameter defaults to false (i.e. no test data subjects will be returned). Exactly 1 item is required.

name.description: This parameter filters results for data subjects that have last given consent from the specific collection point.

values.description: The unique identifier of a collection point that recorded a data subject's last transaction. Minimum 1 item, maximum 20 values.

name.description: This parameter filters results by organization IDs.

values.description: The unique identifier of the organization. Records assigned to the organization and any child organizations below it will be returned.

name.description: This parameter filters results by a specific identifier type for the primary identifier, such as email.

values.description: The type of data subject identifier used as a primary identifier.

name.description: This parameter filters results by purpose IDs.

values.description: The unique identifier of a purpose. Minimum 1 item, maximum 20 values.

name.description: This parameter filters results by purpose IDs.

values.description: The unique identifier of a purpose.

name.description: This parameter filters results by purpose versions.

values.description: The version of the purpose. Minimum 1 item, maximum 20 values.

name.description: This parameter filters results by the source purpose GUID.

values.description: The source purpose GUID from which to copy status. Exactly 1 item is required.

name.description: This parameter filters results by the consent status. For more information, see [Data Subject Purpose Statuses.](https://my.onetrust.com/s/article/UUID-d4ed05a9-a999-f2cb-8272-610a5817b43f?language=en_US#UUID-d4ed05a9-a999-f2cb-8272-610a5817b43f_section-idm46212287084304)

values.description: The status of consent. Minimum 1 item, maximum 20 values.

name.description: This parameter filters results by the target purpose GUID.

values.description: The target purpose GUID to which to copy status. Exactly 1 item is required.

name.description: This parameter filters results by test data subjects.

values.description: This flag indicates whether to include or exclude test data subjects. Exactly 1 item is required.

name.description: This parameter filters results by withdrawal notes.

values.description: The reason notes that explain why consent was withdrawn.

id.description: The unique identifier of the user who initiated the bulk export.

value.description: The name of the user who initiated the bulk export.

valueKey.description: This value will always be null.

displayLabel.description: The display label for the option value.

disabled.description: This flag indicates whether the user is inactive.

name.description: The name of the parameter used to filter results. Available parameters depend on exportType.

values.description:
The values of the parameter used to filter results. Max items depend on parameter name and exportType.

- For CONSENT_RECEIPTS and COOKIE_RECEIPTS: All parameters accept up to 20 values.
- For DATA_SUBJECTS: Most parameters accept up to 20 values, except customPreferenceName, customPreferenceValue, dataElementName, dataElementValue, withdrawalNotes, includeTestDataSubject, and dateType which accept only 1 value.

items.description:
The values of the parameter used to filter results. Max items depend on parameter name and exportType.

- For CONSENT_RECEIPTS and COOKIE_RECEIPTS: All parameters accept up to 20 values.
- For DATA_SUBJECTS: Most parameters accept up to 20 values, except customPreferenceName, customPreferenceValue, dataElementName, dataElementValue, withdrawalNotes, includeTestDataSubject, and dateType which accept only 1 value.

runId.description: The unique identifier for the bulk export.

name.description: The name of the bulk export.

status.description: The current status of the bulk export.

createdDate.description: The date and time that the bulk export was initiated.

createdBy.description: The details of the user who initiated the bulk export.

exportType.description: The type of data to export.

fileFormat.description: The file format of the bulk export.

completionDate.description: The date and time that the bulk export was completed.

expiryDate.description: The date and time that the bulk export file expires.

progressPercentage.description: The percent progress of completion for the bulk export.

availableCredit.description: The number of remaining bulk exports for the account.

recordCount.description: The total number of records included within the bulk export.

startDate.description: The start of a date range used to filter results.

endDate.description: The end of a date range used to filter results.

params.description: The parameters used to filter results.

runId.description: The unique identifier of the bulk export.

name.description: The name of the bulk export.

status.description: The current status of the bulk export.

createdDate.description: The date and time that the bulk export was initiated.

createdBy.description: The details of the user who initiated the bulk export.

exportType.description: The type of data to export.

fileFormat.description: The file format of the bulk export.

completionDate.description: The date and time that the bulk export was completed.

expiryDate.description: The date and time that the bulk export file expires.

content.description: The data of the returned objects.

pageable.description: The configuration parameters of the page.

last.description: This flag indicates whether the current page is the last page of the list.

totalElements.description: The total number of results in the list.

totalPages.description: The total number of pages in the list.

size.description: The number of results per page.

number.description: The page number of the results.

sort.description: The sort criteria that dictates the order of the results.

first.description: This flag indicates whether the current page is the first page of the list.

numberOfElements.description: The number of results on the current page.

empty.description: This flag indicates whether no results exist on the page.

offset.description: The number of results to exclude from the start of the list.

sort.description: The sort criteria that dictates the order of the results.

paged.description: This flag indicates whether the results were paged.

pageSize.description: The number of results per page.

pageNumber.description: The page number of the results.

unpaged.description: This flag indicates whether the results were not paged.

empty.description: This flag indicates whether sort criteria was left undefined.

sorted.description: This flag indicates whether the results were sorted.

unsorted.description: This flag indicates whether the results were not sorted.

id.description: The unique identifier of the exported file to download.

headers.description: The HTTP headers to include in the download request. The headers object contains an 'Authorization' key with a bearer token value.

url.description: The URL of the exported file to download.

fileName.description: The system-generated name of the exported file to download.

Authorization.description: The authorization bearer token required to access the bulk export.

items.description: The details of the exported file.

hasNext.description: This flag indicates whether there are more values to be retrieved.

totalCredits.description: The total number of credits available for the account.

creditsRemaining.description: The number of credits remaining for the account.

creditsUsed.description: The number of credits used for the account.

scopes.BULK_EXPORT: Core Scope gives the user access to create/cancel/view bulk export jobs
